concept: Body / The Lived Whole / The Living Frame
description: The body: the whole living frame a person is, not a machine a person rides. The word every part-name has been waiting for — 'korua' (heart), 'rokai' (back), 'mokura' (belly) now have their whole. Distinct from 'lima' (alive, the quality) and 'lioru' (life, the phenomenon).
sound_symbolism: 'we' is unity, 'lo' the flow of aliveness through it, 'ni' the inward resonance of being inhabited. A whole, flowing, lived-in — the body as home rather than object.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia weloni therilu.' (My body rests.) 'weloni shurano nai.' (The body is safe.) Possession is bare juxtaposition: 'lopia weloni' (the child's body).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mindfulness of the body is the first foundation; the practice was waiting for its noun. The word frames the body as inhabited whole, not owned machine.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The body was the first tool, the first measure, and the first home — the wisdom traditions never spoke of it as separate from its person.
